commit.message:
fix: use curl.exe for binary PBF download in CI test script

Invoke-WebRequest corrupts binary files on Windows due to encoding issues.
curl.exe (bundled with Windows 10+) downloads binaries correctly.
Also adds a size sanity check to catch stale corrupt files.

data/foods.js and sketch.js in the correct load order.

commit.message:
Add 3 orthogonal spot trading strategies: vol mean-reversion, cross-sectional arb, microstructure flow (#7)

* Add Volatility Mean-Reversion strategy with Garman-Klass estimator

Implements a novel spot-only strategy that exploits the mean-reverting
nature of volatility in crypto markets using:

- Garman-Klass (1980) OHLC volatility estimator for efficient vol
  estimation from full bar range data
- Vol z-score gating (short vs long GK vol displacement)
- Bollinger Band / Keltner Channel squeeze detection and release
- Composite mean-reversion signal (RSI + VWAP deviation + vol-regime)
- Bayesian HMM regime gating via MarkovRegimeDetector
- Volatility-target position sizing with ATR stops, drawdown circuit
  breaker, and single-instrument crash guard

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: fix vol-scalar fallback and use Decimal.quantize

- Skip instruments with zero realised vol instead of using vol_target
  as a misleading fallback
- Use Decimal.quantize() instead of Decimal(str(round(float(...)))) for
  cleaner precision control

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Cross-Sectional Statistical Arbitrage strategy

Implement a pure cross-sectional stat-arb strategy that ranks all
instruments against each other using six orthogonal factors (relative
momentum, volume surprise, mean-reversion residual, liquidity premium,
funding carry, volatility discount). Key features:

- Rolling OLS beta decomposition against volume-weighted market return
- Rank-based composite scoring with cross-sectional normalisation
- Beta-hedged long-only portfolio construction
- Markov regime gating (full breadth → flight-to-quality → flat)
- Cross-sectional dispersion filter
- Herfindahl concentration check, ATR stop-losses, drawdown circuit
  breaker, and minimum-rank-change turnover control

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Microstructure / Order Flow strategy with funding rate spillover

Implements Strategy C: a microstructure-focused alpha that derives
high-frequency order-flow proxies from OHLCV data and combines them
with perpetual funding-rate spillover signals.

Key components:
- Volume Imbalance Ratio (VIR) acceleration for buy/sell pressure
- VPIN proxy for flow toxicity gating
- Funding rate contrarian + momentum signals
- Price impact asymmetry detection
- Amihud illiquidity acceleration
- Markov regime gating with four-state exposure scaling
- ATR stop-losses, drawdown circuit breaker, crash guard
- Signal persistence filter (min_signal_bars)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests for vol_mean_reversion, cross_sectional_arb, microstructure_flow strategies

72 tests across 3 files covering:
- Helper function unit tests (GK variance, ATR, vol, buy/sell volume, VPIN)
- Portfolio construction (weight caps, gross exposure, non-negative weights)
- Edge cases (empty input, insufficient data, zero price/volume)
- Regime gating and crisis exposure
- Stop-loss and drawdown circuit breaker
- Factory functions
- BacktestEngine integration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: bound detector head reads

Use a shared bounded streaming helper for detector head reads so container, MCP, and custom-rule detectors never allocate full files just to inspect the first 8 KiB.

Add tests that verify truncation and that bounded reads stop once the limit is reached, even before a stream reaches EOF.

commit.message:
Fix AOT compilation: source-gen JSON for all serialization

- MulticastFeedConfig: replace reflection-based JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>
  with source-generated FeedConfigJsonContext. Replace non-generic
  JsonStringEnumConverter with UseStringEnumConverter in source-gen options.
- Server: enable EnableRequestDelegateGenerator for AOT-compatible MapGet
  delegate binding (eliminates IL2026/IL3050 trim warnings).
- Zero AOT analysis warnings on publish.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: expand reader group to all project resources

Reader group now gets read-only access across every resource
provisioned by Terraform, not just the AKS cluster:

- Subscription: Reader (ARM control-plane visibility on all resources)
- AKS cluster: Azure Kubernetes Service RBAC Reader (k8s data-plane)
- ACR: AcrPull (pull images, no push)
- Key Vault: Key Vault Reader (metadata only, no secret values)

Admin group assignments are unchanged.

Complete portfolio redesign for Jasper Haas

- Replace index.html with full redesigned structure (618 lines)

  - Sections: Hero, About, Projects (5 cards), Skills, Timeline, Contact, Footer

  - CSS custom properties, full reset, sticky nav with backdrop-filter
  - clamp() hero typography, gradient text, animated skill bars
  - CSS-based alternating timeline, glassmorphism contact form
  - Responsive breakpoints (1024px, 768px, 480px)

  - 80 particles (35 on mobile), connecting lines < 150px
  - violet + gold colors, drift + edge-wrap, debounced resize

- Add README.md with Strato upload guide, local preview, file tree

commit.message:
Fix oversized share link handling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Incremental book updates on frontend, revert server-side snapshot spam

Server: revert sending BookSnapshot after every order mutation. Snapshot is
sent only at subscribe time (correct market data pattern).

Frontend: maintain local MBO order map (orderId → {side, price, qty}) and
apply OrderAdded/Updated/Deleted incrementally to MBP levels:
- OrderAdded: track order, add qty to price level
- OrderUpdated: subtract old contribution, apply new values
- OrderDeleted: remove tracked order from price level
- BookSnapshot: reset MBO tracking, set MBP from snapshot
- BookCleared: clear both book and orders

Pre-snapshot orders that get deleted/updated without being tracked accept
minor drift — self-corrects over time or on next snapshot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix cipher suite typo: POLY1205 -> POLY1305 in TLS docs (#5879)

`ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1205` was listed as a valid cipher suite in
three SSL/TLS documentation pages — the correct name is
`ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305`. Copying the incorrect value results in
`SSL_CTX_set_cipher_list:no cipher match` errors.

## Changes

- **`solutions/observability/apm/apm-server/ssl-tls-input-settings.md`**
— corrected cipher name
-
**`solutions/observability/apm/apm-server/ssl-tls-output-settings.md`**
— corrected cipher name
- **`reference/fleet/elastic-agent-ssl-configuration.md`** — corrected
cipher name

All other cipher suite names in these files were verified against the Go
`crypto/tls` source and are correct.

Part of https://github.com/elastic/apm-server/issues/20876

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: carsonip <9133397+carsonip@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Send BookSnapshot after every order mutation

The frontend only renders the book from BookSnapshot messages — OrderAdded/
Updated/Deleted events were logged but never applied to the local book state.
Now the server sends a full BookSnapshot after each order event so the
displayed book stays in sync with the server-side MBO book.

Also sends empty BookSnapshot at subscribe time when book doesn't exist yet
(previous commit), ensuring the counter increments even before orders arrive.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove hardware-clock from statime config; add stale socket cleanup

hardware-clock = "auto" in nightly-2026-04-13 binary broke usrvclock-export
on nodes without hardware PTP timestamping (all nodes except .46/i219-LM).
The ALSA inferno plugin timed out waiting for overlay updates because
statime in hardware-clock mode did not properly initialize the software
usrvclock on non-hardware-PTP nodes.

Fix:
- Remove hardware-clock = "auto" from inferno-ptpv1.toml template
  (pure software PTP is sufficient for audio; hardware PTP had no
  audible quality benefit, only caused regression)
- Add ExecStartPre=-/bin/rm -f /tmp/ptp-usrvclock to statime-inferno.service
  to clear stale sockets on restart (prevents bind() failure and
  fixes .45 which had a March-13 stale socket blocking statime restart)

Hotfixed all 4 nodes (.43, .45, .46, .51) — all audio services active.

commit.message:
Improve simplification skill to reduce false positives

Strengthen parity filter with explicit rules for 1:1 module mapping,
orchestration methods, and protocol-version functions. Add cohesive
module and sequential pipeline exemptions. Raise GOD FUNCTION threshold
from 150 to 200 lines. Refine TRIVIAL WRAPPER, C-STYLE MUTATION,
UNNECESSARY CLONING, and SCATTERED CONCERN filters.

Based on analysis of 36 won't-fix issues: these changes would have
prevented ~34 of them (~94% false positive reduction).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add smoke test workflow with branch protection

Runs bash test/smoke.sh on every PR targeting main and on every push to
main. Requires all 23 smoke tests to pass before a merge is allowed.

Security hardening for a public repository:
- pull_request trigger (not pull_request_target): fork PRs run in a
  restricted context with no secrets and a read-only GITHUB_TOKEN,
  preventing privilege-escalation via workflow modification.
- permissions: {} at workflow level, contents: read at job level —
  no write access granted at any scope.
- actions/checkout pinned to commit SHA (v4.2.2) to prevent
  tag-mutable supply chain attacks.
- timeout-minutes: 15 to prevent runaway jobs.
- concurrency group cancels stale in-progress runs on new pushes.

To enforce this as a merge gate: in repo Settings → Branches, add a
branch protection rule for main requiring the 'Smoke tests' status
check to pass before merging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
